chapters.title: Chapter 2: Glitch in the System
chapters.text:
Haruto trudged through the dense forest, pushing aside oversized ferns and vines that seemed straight out of a fantasy movie. His legs were already aching, and the reality of his situation was starting to sink in. No save points. No respawn. This wasn’t a game he could just alt+F4 out of.

He stopped by a crystal-clear river to drink some water and check his reflection.

“What the—?!”

Staring back at him was a face that looked mostly like his own… except for the hair. Pure white, almost glowing under the dappled sunlight, falling messily over his forehead in that signature anime protagonist style.

Haruto ran a hand through it, eyes wide. “Okay… hair color change on summoning. Classic isekai trope. At least it looks cool.”

He continued walking, trying to keep his cool. A faint dirt path appeared between the trees, and he followed it cautiously.

Then the ground shook.

A massive lion burst from the undergrowth, its mane crackling with electric blue lightning. Golden eyes locked onto him with predatory hunger. Sparks danced along its fur as it roared, sending birds scattering into the sky.

Haruto froze. “This isn’t the tutorial zone! What the hell—this thing’s way above level 1!”

His heart hammered. He remembered all the isekai stories he’d read and played: cheat skills, overpowered abilities, instant mastery.

*Think! Think! What would a hacker protagonist do?*

Suddenly, a calm, mechanical voice echoed directly inside his head:

**[Common Language Acquired.]**  
**[Unique Skill: Hacker Acquired.]**

“Yes!” Haruto pumped his fist. “Hacker class! That’s what I’m talking about—!”

The voice continued without emotion:

**[To activate Hacker, physical contact with target is required. Analyze and modify underlying mana code.]**

Haruto’s excitement died instantly. “…Touch it? With this thing?”

The lightning lion charged.

Haruto spun on his heel and sprinted for his life, dodging between trees, heart pounding in his ears. Branches whipped at his face. The lion was terrifyingly fast—its paws thundered behind him, closing the gap in seconds.

It leaped.

A heavy paw slammed into his back, pinning him face-down into the dirt. Haruto struggled wildly, kicking and twisting, but the weight was crushing.

“Wait—now I can touch it!” he realized.

Desperation fueled him. He twisted one arm free, slapped his palm against the lion’s massive foreleg, and activated the skill with sheer willpower.

**[Analyze Initiated.]**

In his mind’s eye, the lion’s body transformed into streams of glowing code—mana pathways laid out like intricate C++ functions, loops of lightning magic, conditional branches for attack patterns, variables for strength and speed.

Haruto’s fingers twitched as if typing on an invisible keyboard. He spotted a critical vulnerability in the lightning mana loop—a recursive overload point.

He tweaked it.

**[Modification Successful.]**  
**[New Derived Skill: Glitch Acquired.]**

The lion sensed immediate danger and tried to hop back, but it was too late.

Its mane erupted in a violent explosion of uncontrolled lightning. Blue arcs lashed wildly, scorching trees and the ground around them. The beast roared in pain but somehow survived the backlash, charred and furious.

It reared up, claws extended, ready to finish Haruto with one savage strike.

Haruto panicked, squeezing his eyes shut and throwing his arms up. “Not like this—!”

*Schwing!*

A sickening sound of metal slicing through flesh and bone echoed through the forest.

When Haruto opened his eyes, the lion’s head was rolling on the ground, cleanly severed. No blood sprayed—some kind of magic must have cauterized the wound instantly. The massive body collapsed with a heavy thud.

Haruto clapped both hands over his mouth, fighting the urge to vomit. “Oh god… that’s way too realistic…”

A deep, gruff voice called out from behind him.

“You okay, kid?”

An old man stepped out from the trees, wiping a gigantic sword clean on a cloth. He looked like a veteran warrior—scarred face, graying beard, muscular build despite his age, wearing battered leather armor reinforced with metal plates.

Haruto stared at the enormous blade, then back at the beheaded lion.

The old man tilted his head, eyeing Haruto’s school uniform and white hair with curiosity.

“Those clothes… unusual. You one of the legendary Heroes summoned by the kingdom?”

Haruto blinked, still in shock. “Hero…? Me?”

---

**Elsewhere…**

In a bustling frontier town, a boy with now bright red hair wandered the crowded streets, a strange smile playing on his lips. Takeshi—the quiet nerd from Haruto’s class—had always secretly fantasized about being transported to another world where he could finally be the one in control.

A voice whispered in his mind:

**[Unique Skill: Dominate Acquired.]**

Takeshi’s smile widened into something darker, more predatory.

“Finally… this world will kneel.”

He walked forward with newfound confidence, eyes scanning the townsfolk like potential puppets.

---

In a rocky canyon, Ken—the leader of the school delinquents—now sporting spiky orange hair—laughed maniacally as a group of bandits tried to ambush him.

“Big mistake, losers!”

He punched the first bandit square in the chest. The man exploded in a shower of light and gore.

**[Unique Skill: Blast Acquired.]**

Every punch carried explosive force. The remaining bandits turned to run, but Ken was faster. Fists flew. Bodies detonated one after another until the canyon fell silent.

Ken cracked his knuckles, grinning wildly. “This power… I could get used to this.”

---

Deep inside a dimly lit dungeon, a girl with flowing purple hair moved like a shadow. Himari—the quiet, serious girl from their class—sliced through a pack of goblins with cold precision, her katana flashing in the torchlight.

**[Unique Skill: Samurai Acquired.]**

She didn’t smile. She didn’t hesitate. Just efficient, deadly elegance as the last goblin fell.

---

Back in the royal palace of Aldoria, Shinji stood in the grand hall, his hair now a striking golden blonde that made him look every bit the classic hero. He had been given fresh clothes and was listening to the king’s report when a messenger burst in.

“Your Majesty… more unknown people were found. Some dead.”

Shinji’s face paled. He rushed to the courtyard where several bodies had been brought in—classmates caught in the scattered summoning light. Some had clearly been killed by monsters, others by bandits or falls from great heights.

Tears welled in his eyes as he recognized faces.

“They… they died because of the ritual interruption,” the king said solemnly. “The summoning pulled in far more than the intended three heroes. The mana backlash scattered everyone across the continent. We are truly sorry for these losses.”

Shinji clenched his fists, blonde hair falling over his face.

“This world… it’s cruel.”

---

Haruto, still processing everything in the forest, looked up at the old swordsman.

“So… I’m a hero now? Like, with stats and everything?”

The old man chuckled. “Names’s Garrick. Come on, kid. Let’s get you somewhere safe before more beasts show up. You’ve got a lot to learn about this world… Hero.”

Haruto followed, mind racing with possibilities.

*Hacker skill, Glitch skill… If I can touch things to rewrite their code, this world is basically one giant program waiting to be exploited.*

A dangerous, excited glint appeared in his white-haired eyes.

“Alright, Garrick-san. Lead the way. Time to debug this entire reality.”

---

chapters.title: Chapter 3: Black Ninja vs. the Dragon
chapters.text:
“Run faster, you lazy brat! Is that all a Hero can manage?!”

Garrick’s voice boomed behind Haruto like a drill sergeant on steroids. The old swordsman was jogging casually while Haruto sprinted full speed through the forest, sweat pouring down his face, white hair sticking to his forehead.

“I’m… a hacker… not… a marathon runner!” Haruto gasped between breaths.

After the lion incident, Garrick had decided that “training” was non-negotiable. They had spent the entire morning doing sword drills with a wooden practice blade. Haruto kept complaining in his head the whole time.

*Why am I grinding like this? I’m supposed to be the cheat character!*

The answer was brutally simple. Hacking monsters was easy because their magic circuits were relatively straightforward—like basic scripts. Humans (and intelligent beings) had far more complex mana structures. It took too much time to analyze and glitch them in the middle of a fight, and enemies definitely didn’t stand still politely waiting to be touched.

And this was real life. No beautiful elf mage or busty sword saint training him—just a loud old grandpa who smelled faintly of cheap ale and sweat.

*This isekai is rigged,* Haruto thought bitterly as he dodged another swing from Garrick’s practice sword.

---

Deep inside a misty dungeon, another summoned student was having the time of his life.

Minato grinned ear to ear, his newly blue hair shining under the glow of dungeon crystals. The complete otaku of the class had been waiting his entire life for this exact moment. Goblins, slimes, and skeletal warriors fell before him as he activated his unique skill.

**[Copy Skill Acquired: Fireball]**  
**[Copy Skill Acquired: Iron Skin]**

“YES! This is it! The power fantasy I always dreamed of!” he laughed maniacally, copying and combining abilities on the fly.

He wiped monster blood from his cheek and kept pushing deeper. *If Haruto were here, what would he do?* Minato wondered. He was one of the few who actually got along with Haruto, though Haruto mostly kept his distance, preferring to spend lunch breaks coding instead of playing games.

Minato’s eyes sparkled with excitement. *There might be a princess to save somewhere. I need to grind more so I can make a good impression!*

He charged forward with renewed energy, already imagining the heroic entrance he would make.

---

In the royal palace of Aldoria, Princess Elara stood on a high balcony, her long crimson hair fluttering in the breeze. Her sharp blue eyes gazed at the distant horizon with an unreadable expression. Ever since the chaotic hero summoning, she had felt a strange restlessness in her chest.

Footsteps approached. Shinji—now with striking golden-blonde hair—approached and gave a proper, slightly awkward bow.

“Your Highness,” he greeted formally. “His Majesty informed me they found another summoned person. I wish to go to that location and ensure their safety.”

The princess turned, studying him. “Are you keeping up with your training, Hero Shinji?”

Shinji nodded confidently. “Yes. My unique skill, **Justice**, is growing stronger. It draws power from the hope of the people I protect. The more lives I save… the stronger I become.”

Princess Elara smiled softly. “Then I shall accompany you on this journey.”

Shinji’s eyes widened. “It’s dangerous out there—”

“Do not worry,” she interrupted, a playful yet determined glint in her blue eyes. “After all, you’ll be there to protect me, won’t you?”

Shinji couldn’t argue with that.

---

Meanwhile, on the outskirts of a mid-sized border city called Rivermoor, Haruto and Garrick arrived wearing hooded cloaks for disguise.

Haruto had fashioned a kitsune-style ninja mask from scavenged cloth and string, proudly pulling it over his face.

Garrick immediately snatched it off. “Idiot! That thing’s attracting more attention than your white hair!”

“But you said not to show my face!” Haruto protested, twitching.

“Mask’s doing the opposite, kid. You look like a suspicious assassin now.”

Grumbling, Haruto stayed outside the tavern while Garrick went in for “information gathering” (which probably meant drinking). Since his first mask was confiscated, Haruto quickly pulled out a spare one he had made earlier—a sleek black half-mask with a stylized dragon design.

He was adjusting it when chaos erupted.

A massive dragon descended on the city with a deafening roar. Crimson scales gleamed as it breathed torrents of fire, setting rooftops ablaze. Screams filled the air as people fled in panic.

Haruto’s eyes lit up with excitement and adrenaline. He threw on a black robe he’d “borrowed” earlier, pulled the dragon mask firmly over his face, and drew the practice sword Garrick had given him.

“Not on my watch!” he yelled, running toward the dragon. “Don’t worry, citizens! The Black Ninja is here!”

(The name was invented on the spot. He cringed internally but kept running.)

The dragon unleashed another blast of fire. Haruto swung his sword with the technique Garrick had drilled into him that morning. Using **Hacker**, he touched the blade and dismantled the incoming fire magic, converting it into raw mana that charged his sword with crackling energy.

*Schwing!*

The fire split apart harmlessly around him.

People stopped fleeing for a moment, staring in awe. “Who is that?!”

A high-ranking knight who had rushed to the scene narrowed his eyes at Haruto’s movements. The way he used lightning mana to boost his speed and agility… that technique was extremely advanced—something only a select few were taught directly by the royal family.

*Where did this masked boy learn the Thunder Step?* the knight wondered.

Haruto leaped onto the dragon’s back while it tried to shake him off, claws scraping against scales. Clinging tightly, he slapped his palm onto the dragon’s hide and activated **Analyze**.

Streams of complex mana code flooded his mind—vast, ancient, and incredibly dense. He spotted a weak point in the dragon’s magic circulation and attempted a **Glitch** to short-circuit it.

The dragon roared in fury.

Unfortunately, the creature had an enormous mana pool. The glitch backfired spectacularly.

**BOOOOM!**

A massive explosion ripped through the sky. The dragon detonated in a blinding blast of fire and mana, creating a crater the size of half the city. The shockwave flattened nearby buildings, and the resulting fireball could be seen from towns miles away.

Haruto barely managed to jump off and sprint away before the blast engulfed him, rolling across the ground singed and coughing.

Garrick burst out of the tavern just in time to see the devastation. His eyes widened at the giant smoking crater. Then he spotted the royal knight staring suspiciously in their direction.

Quickly pulling the kitsune mask over his own face to hide, Garrick rushed over, grabbed the somewhat injured Haruto by the collar, and dragged him into a side alley.

“You absolute moron!” the old man hissed. “What part of ‘keep a low profile’ did you not understand?!”

Haruto grinned weakly through the soot on his face, still wearing the black dragon mask. “Hey… I saved the city… technically.”

Garrick sighed deeply, already planning their hurried escape from Rivermoor before the knight could track them down.

---

chapters.title: Chapter 4: Stolen Skills and Masked Shadows
chapters.text:
While Haruto was busy turning a dragon into a walking glitch bomb, Shinji and Princess Elara were already on the road.

Their small escort traveled toward Duke City, where rumors spoke of another “Hero” who had appeared recently. Shinji rode beside the princess, still uneasy about bringing her into potential danger, but her determination left no room for argument.

---

In a quiet roadside town not far from their route, terror unfolded.

A panicked classmate sprinted through the narrow streets, breathing hard, eyes wide with fear. He tripped over a loose cobblestone and crashed to the ground.

Before he could scramble up, another figure stepped out from the shadows.

Ryota.

The tall, sharp-featured boy who had been one of Ken’s closest delinquent friends—the same group that had been bullying Takeshi the other day—looked down at his former classmate with cold eyes.

“This is as far as you go,” Ryota said flatly.

“Ryota, please stop! We’re classmates!” the boy begged, crawling backward.

Ryota didn’t listen.

An ice lance materialized in his hand. With a swift thrust, he ended the boy’s life.

**[Unique Skill: Steal Activated.]**  
New skill acquired: **Sewing**

Ryota punched the nearby wall in frustration. “What the fuck? Another trash skill?!” 

He swore under his breath. Most of his classmates had been given completely useless abilities after the summoning. So far, the only decent skills he had stolen were **Ice Magic** and **Healing** from previous victims. Everything else was garbage.

As he walked away from the body, Ryota spotted a small group of riders approaching on the main road. His eyes narrowed, then widened with greed.

One of the riders was Shinji—now with golden-blonde hair, looking every bit the summoned hero. Beside him rode a stunning girl with long crimson hair and sharp blue eyes: Princess Elara.

Ryota’s lips curled into a greedy smile. *Two heroes in one place? Their skills must be insane.*

He acted instantly.

With a wave of his hand, he froze the entire road surface into a slick sheet of ice. The horses slipped and crashed down, throwing their riders. Soldiers scrambled to their feet, drawing weapons.

Shinji was the first to recover, sword already in hand. “Ryota?! What the hell are you doing?!”

Other soldiers charged, but Ryota hurled a barrage of razor-sharp ice shards. Most of them were cut down before they could react.

Shinji stared in disbelief. “You… you were with Ken that day. The one bullying Takeshi.”

Ryota laughed. “Just… collecting skills. That’s all.”

A royal knight commander rose from the fallen horse, armor dented but eyes burning with resolve. “Leave this to me, Hero Shinji.”

The fight erupted.

Ryota formed an ice sword and clashed against the commander’s blade. He had killed dozens of monsters and several knights on his journey, stealing their magic and techniques. His movements were brutally efficient.

Shinji watched in horror as his classmate—someone he had known since middle school—fought on equal footing with a seasoned royal commander using the advanced **Lightning Step** technique.

*How did he get this strong so fast?*

Shinji found an opening and lunged, but at the last second his sword hesitated. *He’s still my classmate…*

That moment of paralysis cost him.

Princess Elara suddenly stepped forward. Lightning crackled around her as she materialized a glowing lightning sword.

Then—

**BOOOOM!**

A massive explosion echoed from far beyond the next city. A colossal pillar of fire and smoke rose into the sky—the aftermath of Haruto’s disastrous dragon glitch.

Everyone froze for a split second.

In that instant, Princess Elara moved.

Her lightning sword flashed with blinding speed. Ryota’s eyes widened, but it was already too late. His head flew from his shoulders before he could even register the attack.

Shinji stood frozen, mouth open in shock. The princess had moved so fast he couldn’t even track her movements.

Ryota’s body collapsed.

Shinji lowered his sword, voice trembling. “He… he was still our classmate…”

Princess Elara sheathed her lightning sword calmly. “He chose his path. I apologize for the trouble your classmate caused, Hero Shinji. But hesitation will only get more people killed in this world.”

Shinji could only nod sadly, guilt heavy in his chest.

---

The group continued to Duke City, where news of the distant “BOOM” had already spread.

They learned it was caused by two masked figures. One had apparently fought a rampaging dragon to protect the city before the explosion occurred.

The royal knight commander met with the local knight who had witnessed the event.

“He used the **Lightning Step** technique,” the knight reported. “Advanced level. Only those personally taught by the royal family should know it.”

The commander’s face went pale with shock. “Impossible… How could an outsider know a royal secret technique?”

Princess Elara stood quietly in the exact spot where Haruto had fought the dragon. She placed a hand over her chest, feeling a strange, familiar warmth—the same sensation she had felt on the day of the chaotic summoning.

Shinji clenched his fist. “We must find these masked people who call themselves ‘Black Ninjas.’ They could be friend… or foe.”

---

In a hidden safehouse on the outskirts of the ruined city, Haruto slowly woke up on a simple cot.

He blinked, looking around the unfamiliar room. Garrick was in the corner, speaking in low voices with a few rough-looking men.

“…and the king must never find out about this,” Garrick was saying.

Haruto sat up quickly. “Wait, old man! You’re not doing something illegal, are you? I have zero intention of becoming an international criminal!”

Garrick sighed deeply and turned around. “Fine. Sit down and listen carefully, kid. This is for the benefit of the country.”

Haruto crossed his arms, still wary. “This better be good.”

---

At the Duke’s grand palace, Shinji and Princess Elara finally arrived.

A girl was sitting elegantly at a long table, sipping tea with perfect posture. She looked up as they entered.

Shinji’s eyes widened in recognition.

“You’re… the class rep!”

---

chapters.title: Chapter 5: Seeds of Domination
chapters.text:
Ken strolled down a dusty street in a remote border town, orange hair catching the sunlight. He was still riding high from the power of his **Blast** skill—every punch an explosion, every fight an easy victory. Life in this new world felt good.

Until a familiar figure stepped out from an alley and blocked his path.

Takeshi.

The nerd who had once been their favorite punching bag now stood with bright red hair and an icy, emotionless stare.

Ken smirked. “Oh? Look who’s here. Still alive, huh?”

Takeshi didn’t smile back. His eyes glowed with dark power.

**[Dominate Skill Activated.]**

Ken suddenly felt an unbearable weight crush down on his mind. His thoughts grew sluggish, his will buckling under an invisible force. His knees trembled.

Takeshi spoke calmly, voice cold as steel. “This is for everything you people did to me. From now on, you will be my slave. You will help me dominate this entire world.”

Ken’s proud delinquent ego fought desperately… then shattered. His eyes went blank. He dropped to one knee, head bowed.

“I will do as you say… Master.”

Takeshi’s lips curled into a satisfied, evil smile. “Good boy.”

---

In the rival Kingdom of Valthor, a tense meeting was underway in the throne room.

A spy knelt before the king, head lowered. “We interfered with Aldoria’s summoning ritual, but we could not fully stop it. The heroes were scattered across their lands. However, as long as the three prophesied heroes do not gather together, they pose no real threat.”

The king nodded grimly. “Very well. We will launch our attack before they can unite.”

The crown prince leaned forward with a cunning grin. “But wait… I want Princess Elara secured and brought to me.”

His younger sister, the second princess with sleek black hair and piercing red eyes, snorted in amusement. She was the same age as Elara and burned with jealousy whenever the Aldorian princess’s beauty was praised in other countries.

“Come on, big brother. You can find another girl to play with. But if you really insist… I can crush her, bring her to you as a gift. You won’t mind if one or two arms or legs get broken, right?”

The prince laughed heartily. “Alright, alright. At least try to bring her in one piece. Once I’ve had my fun with her, you can do whatever you want.”

The second princess’s red eyes narrowed with dark pleasure. She hated how Elara was admired everywhere while their own kingdom—full of shady dealings and dark rumors—struggled to form alliances. It wasn’t that she wasn’t beautiful herself… it was simply that no one trusted Valthor.

---

Back in the Kingdom of Aldoria, King Reginald sat at the war council table, face heavy with worry.

His eldest son asked, “Father, what should we do about the third hero? He is still missing. Only those of royal blood can sense whether a person is truly one of the summoned heroes.”

King Reginald sighed. “We keep searching. But tensions with the neighboring kingdoms are rising dangerously high. We must prepare for war.”

---

In Duke City, inside a quiet room in the duke’s palace, Shinji sat across from the class representative, Rin.

Rin had always been the serious, studious girl in their class. Now her hair had a subtle scholarly sheen, and she carried herself with calm confidence.

“So… how did you end up here?” Shinji asked curiously.

Rin smiled faintly, sipping her tea. “It’s obvious, isn’t it? I love studying. When the summoning light hit, I was in the school library surrounded by books. My unique skill, **Wisdom**, lets me absorb knowledge at an incredible speed. I’ve already learned several complex magic systems in just a few days.”

She paused, then added, “My rapid progress caught the attention of the local mages. Word reached the duke—who happens to be King Reginald’s younger brother. That’s why I’m here now.”

Shinji nodded, impressed. “Your skill sounds really useful. We need every advantage we can get.”

---

In a dark alley far from the palace, a figure moved silently.

Ryota walked with a slight limp, but his wounds were already closing thanks to the stolen **Healing** skill. He remembered the moment he had killed the princess’s escort knights—one of them had been a mage. He had stolen **Illusion Magic** right before escaping death.

He had appraised Shinji during the fight and found him… underwhelming. But he hadn’t bothered to fully appraise the royal knight commander. That man was a real beast.

Ryota’s eyes gleamed with dark ambition as he thought of Princess Elara.

“She won’t go gently into death… but I’ll make sure to take everything she has.”

---

chapters.title: Chapter 6: Telepathic Hack and Unexpected Reunion
chapters.text:
In the opulent halls of Count Patrick’s palace, the count sat at his grand desk, carefully writing a sealed letter. A trusted messenger stood waiting nearby.

Suddenly, a calm voice cut through the silence.

“Are you done with the letter?”

Guards drew their weapons instantly. “Who’s there?!”

From the shadows stepped a figure in a sleek black robe and dragon mask — Haruto.

“Black Ninja,” he announced casually.

Count Patrick’s eyes widened. “Black… Ninja? How did you enter my palace without triggering any alarms?!”

Haruto shrugged. “Top hackers disable the antivirus before infecting the system. In your case, it was the barrier magic. Took a little effort, but I managed somehow.”

The count opened his mouth to shout for more guards, but Haruto moved like lightning — using the **Lightning Step** technique Garrick had taught him. In a blur, he knocked out both the count and the messenger with precise strikes, then dragged their unconscious bodies out of the palace.

Nearby, at a modest inn on the outskirts, Garrick was still meticulously planning an infiltration when a window suddenly knocked open. Two bodies were unceremoniously dropped inside.

Garrick stared in stunned silence.

Haruto dusted off his hands. “Your planning was too slow for my patience.”

The old man quickly read the letter the count had been writing. His face darkened.

“They’re planning to abduct the princess… with traitors inside the royal palace.”

Haruto nodded. “Alright. I’m heading to the royal palace now.”

Garrick stopped him. “Wait — how will you recognize the traitor?”

Haruto tapped the side of his head with a smirk. “Once you make the count open his mouth, tell me everything. I’ll reach the capital and we can talk telepathically. I’ll keep the line open.”

Garrick was stunned. “Telepathically…? How far have you pushed that skill already?”

Haruto grinned under the mask. As someone who already understood radio waves, signal encoding, and data transmission, all he had to do was replace technology with magic. Mana made an excellent carrier wave.

“Leave it to me, old man.”

With that, Haruto vanished into the night, heading toward the capital at high speed.

---

In a certain lively town, outside a bustling brothel, Ken knelt on all fours like a dog, thick rope tied around his neck.

Takeshi, red hair shining with wicked confidence, was inside enjoying the company of several beautiful girls, laughing loudly. Every so often he would tug the rope, forcing Ken to bark or perform humiliating tricks for the amusement of onlookers.

Ken’s proud delinquent spirit was completely gone — buried under the overwhelming power of **Dominate**.

---

At the royal palace in Aldoria’s capital, Rin had finally arrived with Shinji and Princess Elara.

The first thing the class rep did was head straight for the royal library, eyes sparkling with excitement as she devoured ancient tomes with her **Wisdom** skill.

Later, while Shinji went to the knights’ training grounds to hone his **Justice** skill, Rin and Princess Elara sat together in a sunny courtyard, drinking tea and having a light, girly chat.

A soft knock sounded on the door.

A woman in her twenties entered — the kingdom’s top royal mage, elegant and sharp-eyed.

“Forgive the interruption, Your Highness. I wanted to ask if any of you know anything about the magic that caused that giant crater in the border city the other day.”

Before anyone could answer, Princess Elara suddenly placed a hand over her chest. A familiar, warm sensation pulsed inside her — the same feeling from the summoning day.

Without a word, she stood up and began walking, as if drawn by an invisible thread.

---

Deep in a western forest, a chubby classmate was sleeping peacefully against a tree, snoring loudly.

A massive dragon landed nearby, drooling as it prepared to make a meal of the sleeping boy.

Just as the dragon opened its jaws, a drop of saliva landed on the boy’s face.

He woke up instantly.

“Oh? Food came to me this time?”

A few minutes later, only a pile of cleanly picked bones remained of the dragon. The chubby boy — Chojoro — licked his fingers happily.

Another classmate emerged from the bushes. “So you’re hungry as always, Chojoro.”

Chojoro looked up. “Oh, it’s you, Yuta.”

The two talked briefly. Yuta explained his plan while Chojoro listened, still chewing on a dragon rib.

Chojoro’s unique skill was **Gluttony** — he could eat literally anything and convert it into power.

Yuta made a simple proposal: “Join me. I’ll provide you with unlimited food.”

Chojoro agreed without a second thought. “Deal!”

---

Haruto finally reached the capital. Using a quick **Hex** hack on the outer barrier, he slipped inside without triggering any alarms.

The moment his foot crossed the threshold, however, he felt someone behind him.

He turned.

A beautiful girl with long crimson hair stood there — Princess Elara.

Haruto tilted his head. “Who are you?”

She didn’t reply. Instead, she walked straight up to him and pulled him into a tight hug, whispering softly against his chest:

“I finally found you…”

Haruto’s face turned bright red under the mask. “W-what happened—?!”

Before he could process the sudden embrace, another calm voice cut through the moment.

“So… you are here too, Haruto.”

A cold shiver ran down Haruto’s spine.

He looked over and saw a girl with neat pink hair and glasses standing a few steps away — it was none other than the class rep, Rin.

She adjusted her glasses with a small smile.

“You came all the way here without completing your homework? Shinji told me that.”

Haruto froze, completely speechless for the first time since arriving in this world.

---

chapters.title: Chapter 7: Decompiled Flames
chapters.text:
Himari walked alone along a quiet forest path, her purple hair swaying gently with each step. A wide river soon blocked her way. On the opposite bank, a group of travelers stood helplessly — the old stone bridge had collapsed into the rushing water.

The people waved desperately, asking for help. Himari didn’t even slow down. Her hand rested lightly on the hilt of her katana.

Before anyone could react, she drew her blade in one fluid motion.

*Schwing!*

The water split cleanly in two, forming a temporary path of dry riverbed as if the river itself had been sliced apart. Himari crossed without a single word or glance, sheathing her sword the moment she reached the other side.

The travelers stood dumbfounded, mouths open. They quickly hurried across the temporary gap before the waters crashed back together. They tried to thank her, bowing repeatedly, but Himari paid them no mind and simply continued walking, cold and distant as ever.

---

In the rival Kingdom of Valthor, war preparations were in full swing. Blacksmiths hammered armor, soldiers drilled in formation, and supply wagons rolled endlessly through the gates.

A figure in a black robe approached the throne room — Yuta.

His unique skill, **Greed**, had shown him a clear truth: he would never gain true glory or power as long as the three main prophesied heroes remained alive. So he had chosen the smarter path — joining the enemy nation for his own benefit.

The Valthor king and his advisors listened with growing surprise as Yuta spoke. He knew far more than their best spies.

“I know the three heroes well,” Yuta said calmly. “Shinji, Rin, and Haruto. I can tell you their abilities.”

He revealed everything he had learned — Shinji’s **Justice** skill that grew stronger with people’s hope, Rin’s **Wisdom** that let her absorb knowledge instantly, and Haruto’s mysterious **Hacker** skill.

“Shinji won’t be much trouble,” Yuta continued. “He hates underhanded tactics and is easy to manipulate or kill in the right situation. Rin and Haruto will be far more bothersome. Especially Haruto.”

He explained how another classmate, Kaeri (who could read minds), had tried to probe Haruto but ran into what felt like an always-active antivirus — Haruto’s hacking skill constantly on high alert.

“Currently, I have four classmates with me,” Yuta added. “Including Chojoro with his **Gluttony** and Kaeri.”

The Valthor king smiled darkly. “You will be well rewarded for this information.”

---

Back in the Kingdom of Aldoria’s royal palace, Haruto sat stiffly on a luxurious sofa. Princess Elara sat unusually close beside him, her crimson hair brushing his shoulder. King Reginald, Shinji, and Rin were also present.

Rin did nothing but stare intently at how close the princess had gotten to Haruto.

Shinji finally broke the awkward silence. “It’s been a while, Haruto. I never expected you to be one of the heroes.”

Rin pushed up her glasses. “Yes, it is obvious. Someone like him cannot possibly be a real hero. There must be some mistake.”

Shinji smirked. “Shouldn’t you be reading books in the library? Up until now you never cared about meetings, but now you’re here. Oh, don’t tell me — you can’t leave Haruto out of your sight. Even when he wasn’t here, you talked about him a lot.”

Rin’s face turned bright red. “I was never—! Shut up!”

Haruto’s heart pounded wildly. *What the hell is going on here?!*

King Reginald cleared his throat. “Haruto, do you know anything about the giant crater that appeared in the border city recently?”

Haruto shot up from the sofa. “No! That wasn’t me! It was a dragon that… self-exploded.”

Everyone except Princess Elara looked shocked.

“So it really was you…” Shinji muttered.

Elara simply smiled softly, as if she had expected it all along.

---

The next moment, they were all standing in the royal training grounds.

“Why did you bring me here?” Haruto asked, annoyed.

Rin adjusted her glasses with a serious expression. “It is to teach you a lesson about carelessness.”

The royal knight commander stepped forward. “Mock battle between Hero Haruto and Hero Rin — begin!”

Haruto glanced at Shinji for help, but Shinji just looked away awkwardly, pretending he couldn’t do anything.

Rin began chanting calmly: “O fire, come forth! Incinerate my enemy with your flames!”

As she chanted, Haruto quietly activated a barrier he called **Memory Viewer**. In his mind, all magic appeared as clean binary code and mana structures. He could see every change happening inside Rin as well.

Rin finished her chant and released a giant fireball.

Haruto dodged effortlessly with **Lightning Step**, moving in a burst of crackling speed.

King Reginald’s eyes widened in amazement. “He can really use the Lightning Step…!”

Haruto smirked. “Function is ready.”

He simply said “Fireball” — and an identical giant fireball appeared in his hand, launched straight at Rin without any incantation.

Rin was taken aback. “How can he use it without chanting?!”

She quickly tried to chant a shield spell, but it was too late. The fireball was already flying toward her.

Shinji suddenly stepped in, blocking the spell with his sword. He had learned from the fight with Ryota — never space out in battle.

Rin yelled in disbelief, “How did you copy my spell?! Or rather, how did you even use it without incantation?!”

Haruto shrugged casually, as if it were the most obvious thing in the world.

“I decompiled your magic and created a function — like in Python. Now I can call it anytime without the full incantation.”

The entire training ground fell silent.

Rin stared at him, glasses slightly askew, face a mix of shock and frustration.

---

chapters.title: Chapter 8: Virus in the Palace
chapters.text:
The training grounds fell into stunned silence after Haruto casually explained how he had decompiled and turned Rin’s fireball into a reusable Python-like function.

King Reginald, however, couldn’t hold back any longer. His eyes were fixed on Haruto with intense curiosity.

“Where exactly did you learn the Lightning Step technique?”

Haruto scratched his white hair casually. “I was taught by an old man. What was his name again… Gar— um, Garrick. Yeah, Garrick.”

A collective shout echoed through the training area.

“WHAAAAAT?!”

The royal knight commander stepped forward, voice shaking. “So… he really survived.”

King Reginald’s face paled with shock and something like relief. “It was my father who found him during the summoning chaos. No wonder you know so much. Garrick was the former king — my father. He was supposed to have died in the last major assassination attempt, but he survived and went into hiding to root out the traitors within the kingdom.”

The king looked at Haruto urgently. “Where is he now?”

“He’s making Count Patrick confess the names of the other traitors,” Haruto replied.

The king nodded. “Has he told you anything yet?”

“Not directly,” Haruto said, then smirked under his serious expression. “But I already found the traitors.”

He held up a small, wriggling red worm-like construct made of pure mana.

Shinji tilted his head. “What is that?”

“It’s a virus. Magic malware I created to scan and infect. I found three of them active in this palace.” Haruto pointed toward a maid standing nervously near the wall. “And one of them is her.”

The moment the words left his mouth, the maid’s eyes widened in panic. She bolted for the door.

Too late.

Haruto had already raised a silent barrier, sealing every exit. The maid slammed into the invisible wall and collapsed.

“From the moment I first stepped into the capital,” Haruto explained calmly, “I was already running scans and blocking escape routes.”

He continued, “I also found someone else from our class — Kaeri. She tried to read my mind to steal knowledge, but my Hacker skill blocked her like an antivirus and reverse-tracked her location. She’s currently in the Valthor Kingdom.”

Everyone in the room froze in shock.

“Our own classmates… chose to oppose us,” Shinji muttered, fists clenched.

Princess Elara’s blue eyes darkened with quiet anger.

---

Meanwhile, in a bustling town not far from the capital, Minato — blue hair messy from endless dungeon grinding — finally arrived, dreaming of the classic isekai life.

*Finally! Time to meet the princess, build a harem, and live the protagonist dream!*

His mind was practically drooling at the thought.

Suddenly, he spotted a familiar sign. “Oh… it’s a brothel.”

He shook his head firmly. “No! I’m the protagonist of this isekai anime. I mustn’t do anything like that. What would the viewers think if they saw me?!”

He was about to turn away when he noticed Ken tied up outside like a dog, barking on command.

Curious, Minato walked closer.

Then Takeshi stepped out of the brothel, red hair gleaming, looking completely at ease.

Minato’s eyes widened. “Of all people… Takeshi?!”

But the moment he met Takeshi’s cold, domineering gaze, a chill ran down his spine.

Takeshi unleashed his **Dominate** skill, sending a heavy wave of mental pressure toward Minato.

Minato resisted, pushing back with his own growing power. “It… won’t work on me!”

Takeshi’s lips curled. “Interesting. You managed to resist.”

He fired several dark energy balls at Minato, hoping to weaken him. At the same time, he ordered Ken to attack as well.

Minato began fighting back, copying whatever minor skills he could, but at his current level he couldn’t fully steal their powerful abilities yet.

The battle raged violently in the street, drawing panicked screams from onlookers as spells and explosions lit up the area.

---

Back at the royal palace, after the traitors had been rounded up and dealt with, Haruto was sleeping soundly in a luxurious guest bed, exhausted from the day’s events.

Princess Elara sat quietly beside him, gently watching his peaceful sleeping face, her crimson hair glowing softly in the moonlight.

The door creaked open. Class rep Rin sneaked inside, trying to be stealthy.

She froze when she saw Elara already there.

“P-Princess?! Why are you here?!”

Elara giggled softly. “Just doing what you came here for?”

Rin’s eyes shot open wide, face turning scarlet. “N-No! I didn’t come to peep on him or anything like that!”

The princess only giggled more.

Then her expression suddenly turned cold and serious. She stood up and walked right up to Rin, leaning close.

“Dear Rin… I have decided to become his wife. I don’t mind if you become his second or third or whatever, but I won’t appreciate it if you bad-mouth him. Got it?”

Rin tried to protest. “S-Second or third—?!”

But when she looked into Elara’s eyes, she realized the princess was completely serious.

Elara gave one last gentle smile and left the room, closing the door softly behind her.

Rin stood there for a long moment, heart racing. Then she slowly walked closer to the sleeping Haruto. Her cheeks burned bright red as she stared at his white hair and relaxed face.

She whispered very quietly, almost to herself, “Idiot…”

---

chapters.title: Chapter 9: Morning Chaos and Charmed Betrayal
chapters.text:
Haruto’s eyes snapped open to the feeling of warmth on both sides of him.

He froze.

Princess Elara was sleeping peacefully on his right, her crimson hair spilled across the pillow like silk. On his left, Class Rep Rin was curled up with her pink hair slightly messy, glasses neatly placed on the bedside table, one hand lightly clutching the edge of his sleeve.

Haruto’s brain short-circuited.

“WHAT THE HELL?!”

He bolted upright, yelling at the top of his lungs.

Elara stirred first, blinking sleepily before a soft, radiant smile spread across her face.

“Oh… good morning, my dearest husband~”

She leaned in as if to kiss his cheek.

Haruto’s face exploded into a deep shade of red. “H-HUSBAND?! Since when—?!”

Before he could finish the sentence, Rin woke up with a startled squeak. The moment she realized the situation, her entire face turned scarlet. She let out a high-pitched “Kyaa—!” and scrambled off the bed, running toward the door while covering her face with both hands.

“I-I didn’t mean to fall asleep here! This is all a misunderstanding!” she cried, slamming the door behind her as she fled down the hallway.

Elara giggled, clearly amused, and hugged Haruto’s arm tighter. “She’s so cute when she’s flustered.”

Haruto’s heart was hammering so hard he thought it might explode. *This isekai is broken… way too many flags at once!*

Then, suddenly, something pinged in his mind.

His magical malware — the red worm virus he had released across the palace — had spread far beyond the capital walls. It had reached a group of shady figures in dark robes hiding in a forest clearing several kilometers away. Through the virus link, he could see fragments of their conversation and mana signatures.

“Someone’s watching us…” one of the robed figures muttered.

Haruto narrowed his eyes. “Trouble incoming.”

---

Meanwhile, outside the town, the fierce battle between Minato and Takeshi was still raging.

Explosions shook the street. Minato was breathing hard, copying whatever small skills he could while dodging Ken’s explosive punches and Takeshi’s dark energy blasts.

Suddenly, a seductive voice echoed from above.

“My my… look who’s here.”

A beautiful woman in revealing robes descended gracefully. Horns curved elegantly from her head and a thin tail swayed behind her — a succubus clearly sent by the Valthor Kingdom.

She smiled with dangerous charm and released a powerful wave of pink mist.

**Charm Magic: Full Activation.**

Both Takeshi and Ken froze, eyes glazing over as the succubus’s control took hold.

Minato, however, felt the mental intrusion and immediately recognized the danger. “Not good—!”

He broke free at the last second and sprinted away as fast as his legs could carry him.

Behind him, dozens of townspeople and hidden agents who had already fallen under the succubus’s charm suddenly attacked, eyes blank and movements mechanical.

Minato barely managed to escape, using every copied skill he had to create distractions and slip through the crowd. He disappeared into the forest, heart pounding.

But Takeshi and Ken remained completely under her control now, kneeling obediently before the succubus.

She licked her lips with satisfaction. “Two new toys for the kingdom… how delightful.”

---

Back at the royal palace, Haruto gently pried himself from Elara’s hug and stood up, expression serious.

“Something’s wrong. My virus just picked up movement outside the capital. And I think one of our classmates is already in trouble.”

Princess Elara’s playful demeanor vanished instantly, replaced by the sharp gaze of a royal who understood the weight of war.

“Then we move,” she said firmly. “Together.”

Haruto nodded, white hair falling over his eyes as he activated his **Hacker** interface in his mind, already preparing new countermeasures.

The real war was about to begin.

---

chapters.title: Chapter 1: The Summoning Glitch
chapters.text:
Haruto slumped back in his gaming chair, the glow of his monitor casting sharp shadows across his messy room. He had just bought the latest hyped RPG, *Eternal Realms: Chronicles of the Veil*, after seeing endless trailers full of epic battles and mysterious lore. For the first fifteen minutes, he tried playing it legit—dodging enemy combos, managing mana, grinding through the tutorial dungeon like a normal player.

It was too hard. Annoyingly hard.

“Screw this,” he muttered, fingers flying across the keyboard. He pulled up a trainer he’d found on a shady forum, entered a few cheat codes, and suddenly the game bent to his will. Infinite health, one-hit kills, max level from the start. He uncovered every hidden chest, every secret boss, every obscure side quest that normal players would take weeks to find. By the time the sun rose, he had platinumed the game—main story, all endings, even the ultra-rare “True Sovereign” route that required solving an ancient cipher in the game’s files.

Still… something felt missing.

Haruto cracked his knuckles, minimized the game, and opened a hex editor. He decompiled chunks of the executable, digging through layers of obfuscated code like it was just another Tuesday. He found the hidden flag for a secret ending no one on the internet had datamined yet: a bittersweet scene where the hero refuses the throne and walks away into the void, hinting at an even deeper multiverse layer.

“Nice,” he whispered, a satisfied grin spreading across his face. “But they really tried to hide that one.”

The next day at school, Haruto couldn’t resist bragging.

“I finished *Eternal Realms* in one night,” he announced loudly in the classroom during break. “All endings. Even the secret one.”

Some classmates cheered and asked for tips. Others groaned in annoyance.

“Dude, you cheated, didn’t you?” one guy muttered.

Haruto just shrugged with a cocky smile. “Skill issue.”

In the far corner of the schoolyard, trouble was brewing. A group of delinquents—tattooed arms, messy hair, the usual suspects—had cornered a scrawny nerd who was clutching his bag like a shield. They were shoving him around, demanding lunch money or just looking for someone to bully.

“Leave him alone!” 

Shinji stepped in. He wasn’t particularly strong or popular, but he had this stubborn sense of justice that always got him in trouble. The delinquents turned on him instantly, laughing as they grabbed his collar and pinned him against the wall.

“Big hero, huh? Mind your own business.”

Before things could escalate further, Haruto came jogging over, panting slightly, his uniform jacket half-unbuttoned.

“Hey! Shinji! Nerd guy!” he called out hurriedly. “Did you guys finish the math homework? I didn’t—spent all night on that game. Come on, let me copy real quick.”

The delinquents stared at him for a second… then burst out laughing.

“This idiot’s worried about homework?” one of them snorted.

“Pathetic. Let’s go, guys. Not worth it.”

They released Shinji with a final shove and walked off, still chuckling at Haruto’s perfectly timed, perfectly lame interruption. Shinji rubbed his neck, giving Haruto a grateful but exasperated look.

“You really didn’t do the homework again?”

“Priorities, man. That secret ending was worth it.”

The rest of the day passed normally—classes, half-hearted note-taking, avoiding teachers’ questions. Just another ordinary slice of high school life.

Until it wasn’t.

---

Far away, in a world torn apart by endless war, the Kingdom of Aldoria stood on the brink of collapse. Demon armies marched from the north, beast tribes raided from the east, and internal nobles plotted in the shadows. King Reginald had no choice left.

“Prepare the Hero Summoning Ritual,” he commanded, voice heavy with exhaustion. “We will call forth three champions from the prophesied realm, as the ancient texts foretold.”

In the grand summoning chamber beneath the royal palace, royal mages chanted in unison. Arcane circles glowed with blinding azure light. The air hummed with raw mana. Three pillars of light began to form—portals meant to pull legendary heroes from another world.

But the ritual was never meant to be peaceful.

A squad of black-armored knights—traitors loyal to a rival faction—burst into the chamber mid-chant. Swords clashed against mage guards. Spells exploded. One of the attackers hurled a disruption artifact straight into the center of the formation.

The light fractured.

Instead of three stable pillars, the magic went wild. The summoning circle shattered into countless fragments of light that scattered across the entire kingdom and beyond, like shooting stars gone rogue.

When the chaos finally died down, only one figure stood in the center of the ruined circle.

A teenage boy in a slightly disheveled school uniform, blinking in confusion.

Shinji.

The royal mages stared in stunned silence. The head mage’s eyes widened as he scanned the residual mana traces with a detection spell.

“The connection… it was split. The other two heroes were scattered far away. This one is the only one who arrived at the intended location.”

Shinji looked around at the armored knights, the glowing runes, the exhausted king watching from the throne balcony.

“Uh… where am I? And why does it smell like ozone and old books?”

---

Meanwhile, deep in an ancient forest far from the capital, another pillar of fading light deposited its passenger with a soft thud.

Haruto landed on a bed of moss, still holding his school bag. He blinked once. Twice.

Tall, unfamiliar trees towered overhead. Sunlight filtered through leaves in shades of emerald he’d never seen in Japan. The air smelled fresh—too fresh, like no pollution had ever touched it. Birds with strangely vibrant plumage chirped overhead.

He sat up slowly, patting himself down. No injuries. Phone was dead (no signal, obviously). Bag still had his notebooks, a half-eaten snack, and… weirdly, his gaming laptop had come with him, though the screen was off.

Haruto stared at the forest for a long moment, processing.

Then a small, reluctant smile crept onto his face.

“Well… at least I survived another day without turning in that math homework.”

He stood up, dusted off his uniform, and looked around with the calm confidence of someone who had just beaten a game on the hardest difficulty using every exploit available.

“Alright, system. Status window. Inventory. Let’s see what kind of broken mechanics this world has.”

Somewhere in the distance, a low growl echoed through the trees.

Haruto cracked his knuckles.

“Time to decompile this new game.”

---

chapters.title: Chapter 2: Glitch in the System
chapters.text:
Haruto trudged through the dense forest, pushing aside oversized ferns and vines that seemed straight out of a fantasy movie. His legs were already aching, and the reality of his situation was starting to sink in. No save points. No respawn. This wasn’t a game he could just alt+F4 out of.

He stopped by a crystal-clear river to drink some water and check his reflection.

“What the—?!”

Staring back at him was a face that looked mostly like his own… except for the hair. Pure white, almost glowing under the dappled sunlight, falling messily over his forehead in that signature anime protagonist style.

Haruto ran a hand through it, eyes wide. “Okay… hair color change on summoning. Classic isekai trope. At least it looks cool.”

He continued walking, trying to keep his cool. A faint dirt path appeared between the trees, and he followed it cautiously.

Then the ground shook.

A massive lion burst from the undergrowth, its mane crackling with electric blue lightning. Golden eyes locked onto him with predatory hunger. Sparks danced along its fur as it roared, sending birds scattering into the sky.

Haruto froze. “This isn’t the tutorial zone! What the hell—this thing’s way above level 1!”

His heart hammered. He remembered all the isekai stories he’d read and played: cheat skills, overpowered abilities, instant mastery.

*Think! Think! What would a hacker protagonist do?*

Suddenly, a calm, mechanical voice echoed directly inside his head:

**[Common Language Acquired.]**  
**[Unique Skill: Hacker Acquired.]**

“Yes!” Haruto pumped his fist. “Hacker class! That’s what I’m talking about—!”

The voice continued without emotion:

**[To activate Hacker, physical contact with target is required. Analyze and modify underlying mana code.]**

Haruto’s excitement died instantly. “…Touch it? With this thing?”

The lightning lion charged.

Haruto spun on his heel and sprinted for his life, dodging between trees, heart pounding in his ears. Branches whipped at his face. The lion was terrifyingly fast—its paws thundered behind him, closing the gap in seconds.

It leaped.

A heavy paw slammed into his back, pinning him face-down into the dirt. Haruto struggled wildly, kicking and twisting, but the weight was crushing.

“Wait—now I can touch it!” he realized.

Desperation fueled him. He twisted one arm free, slapped his palm against the lion’s massive foreleg, and activated the skill with sheer willpower.

**[Analyze Initiated.]**

In his mind’s eye, the lion’s body transformed into streams of glowing code—mana pathways laid out like intricate C++ functions, loops of lightning magic, conditional branches for attack patterns, variables for strength and speed.

Haruto’s fingers twitched as if typing on an invisible keyboard. He spotted a critical vulnerability in the lightning mana loop—a recursive overload point.

He tweaked it.

**[Modification Successful.]**  
**[New Derived Skill: Glitch Acquired.]**

The lion sensed immediate danger and tried to hop back, but it was too late.

Its mane erupted in a violent explosion of uncontrolled lightning. Blue arcs lashed wildly, scorching trees and the ground around them. The beast roared in pain but somehow survived the backlash, charred and furious.

It reared up, claws extended, ready to finish Haruto with one savage strike.

Haruto panicked, squeezing his eyes shut and throwing his arms up. “Not like this—!”

*Schwing!*

A sickening sound of metal slicing through flesh and bone echoed through the forest.

When Haruto opened his eyes, the lion’s head was rolling on the ground, cleanly severed. No blood sprayed—some kind of magic must have cauterized the wound instantly. The massive body collapsed with a heavy thud.

Haruto clapped both hands over his mouth, fighting the urge to vomit. “Oh god… that’s way too realistic…”

A deep, gruff voice called out from behind him.

“You okay, kid?”

An old man stepped out from the trees, wiping a gigantic sword clean on a cloth. He looked like a veteran warrior—scarred face, graying beard, muscular build despite his age, wearing battered leather armor reinforced with metal plates.

Haruto stared at the enormous blade, then back at the beheaded lion.

The old man tilted his head, eyeing Haruto’s school uniform and white hair with curiosity.

“Those clothes… unusual. You one of the legendary Heroes summoned by the kingdom?”

Haruto blinked, still in shock. “Hero…? Me?”

---

**Elsewhere…**

In a bustling frontier town, a boy with now bright red hair wandered the crowded streets, a strange smile playing on his lips. Takeshi—the quiet nerd from Haruto’s class—had always secretly fantasized about being transported to another world where he could finally be the one in control.

A voice whispered in his mind:

**[Unique Skill: Dominate Acquired.]**

Takeshi’s smile widened into something darker, more predatory.

“Finally… this world will kneel.”

He walked forward with newfound confidence, eyes scanning the townsfolk like potential puppets.

---

In a rocky canyon, Ken—the leader of the school delinquents—now sporting spiky orange hair—laughed maniacally as a group of bandits tried to ambush him.

“Big mistake, losers!”

He punched the first bandit square in the chest. The man exploded in a shower of light and gore.

**[Unique Skill: Blast Acquired.]**

Every punch carried explosive force. The remaining bandits turned to run, but Ken was faster. Fists flew. Bodies detonated one after another until the canyon fell silent.

Ken cracked his knuckles, grinning wildly. “This power… I could get used to this.”

---

Deep inside a dimly lit dungeon, a girl with flowing purple hair moved like a shadow. Himari—the quiet, serious girl from their class—sliced through a pack of goblins with cold precision, her katana flashing in the torchlight.

**[Unique Skill: Samurai Acquired.]**

She didn’t smile. She didn’t hesitate. Just efficient, deadly elegance as the last goblin fell.

---

Back in the royal palace of Aldoria, Shinji stood in the grand hall, his hair now a striking golden blonde that made him look every bit the classic hero. He had been given fresh clothes and was listening to the king’s report when a messenger burst in.

“Your Majesty… more unknown people were found. Some dead.”

Shinji’s face paled. He rushed to the courtyard where several bodies had been brought in—classmates caught in the scattered summoning light. Some had clearly been killed by monsters, others by bandits or falls from great heights.

Tears welled in his eyes as he recognized faces.

“They… they died because of the ritual interruption,” the king said solemnly. “The summoning pulled in far more than the intended three heroes. The mana backlash scattered everyone across the continent. We are truly sorry for these losses.”

Shinji clenched his fists, blonde hair falling over his face.

“This world… it’s cruel.”

---

Haruto, still processing everything in the forest, looked up at the old swordsman.

“So… I’m a hero now? Like, with stats and everything?”

The old man chuckled. “Names’s Garrick. Come on, kid. Let’s get you somewhere safe before more beasts show up. You’ve got a lot to learn about this world… Hero.”

Haruto followed, mind racing with possibilities.

*Hacker skill, Glitch skill… If I can touch things to rewrite their code, this world is basically one giant program waiting to be exploited.*

A dangerous, excited glint appeared in his white-haired eyes.

“Alright, Garrick-san. Lead the way. Time to debug this entire reality.”

---

chapters.title: Chapter 3: Black Ninja vs. the Dragon
chapters.text:
“Run faster, you lazy brat! Is that all a Hero can manage?!”

Garrick’s voice boomed behind Haruto like a drill sergeant on steroids. The old swordsman was jogging casually while Haruto sprinted full speed through the forest, sweat pouring down his face, white hair sticking to his forehead.

“I’m… a hacker… not… a marathon runner!” Haruto gasped between breaths.

After the lion incident, Garrick had decided that “training” was non-negotiable. They had spent the entire morning doing sword drills with a wooden practice blade. Haruto kept complaining in his head the whole time.

*Why am I grinding like this? I’m supposed to be the cheat character!*

The answer was brutally simple. Hacking monsters was easy because their magic circuits were relatively straightforward—like basic scripts. Humans (and intelligent beings) had far more complex mana structures. It took too much time to analyze and glitch them in the middle of a fight, and enemies definitely didn’t stand still politely waiting to be touched.

And this was real life. No beautiful elf mage or busty sword saint training him—just a loud old grandpa who smelled faintly of cheap ale and sweat.

*This isekai is rigged,* Haruto thought bitterly as he dodged another swing from Garrick’s practice sword.

---

Deep inside a misty dungeon, another summoned student was having the time of his life.

Minato grinned ear to ear, his newly blue hair shining under the glow of dungeon crystals. The complete otaku of the class had been waiting his entire life for this exact moment. Goblins, slimes, and skeletal warriors fell before him as he activated his unique skill.

**[Copy Skill Acquired: Fireball]**  
**[Copy Skill Acquired: Iron Skin]**

“YES! This is it! The power fantasy I always dreamed of!” he laughed maniacally, copying and combining abilities on the fly.

He wiped monster blood from his cheek and kept pushing deeper. *If Haruto were here, what would he do?* Minato wondered. He was one of the few who actually got along with Haruto, though Haruto mostly kept his distance, preferring to spend lunch breaks coding instead of playing games.

Minato’s eyes sparkled with excitement. *There might be a princess to save somewhere. I need to grind more so I can make a good impression!*

He charged forward with renewed energy, already imagining the heroic entrance he would make.

---

In the royal palace of Aldoria, Princess Elara stood on a high balcony, her long crimson hair fluttering in the breeze. Her sharp blue eyes gazed at the distant horizon with an unreadable expression. Ever since the chaotic hero summoning, she had felt a strange restlessness in her chest.

Footsteps approached. Shinji—now with striking golden-blonde hair—approached and gave a proper, slightly awkward bow.

“Your Highness,” he greeted formally. “His Majesty informed me they found another summoned person. I wish to go to that location and ensure their safety.”

The princess turned, studying him. “Are you keeping up with your training, Hero Shinji?”

Shinji nodded confidently. “Yes. My unique skill, **Justice**, is growing stronger. It draws power from the hope of the people I protect. The more lives I save… the stronger I become.”

Princess Elara smiled softly. “Then I shall accompany you on this journey.”

Shinji’s eyes widened. “It’s dangerous out there—”

“Do not worry,” she interrupted, a playful yet determined glint in her blue eyes. “After all, you’ll be there to protect me, won’t you?”

Shinji couldn’t argue with that.

---

Meanwhile, on the outskirts of a mid-sized border city called Rivermoor, Haruto and Garrick arrived wearing hooded cloaks for disguise.

Haruto had fashioned a kitsune-style ninja mask from scavenged cloth and string, proudly pulling it over his face.

Garrick immediately snatched it off. “Idiot! That thing’s attracting more attention than your white hair!”

“But you said not to show my face!” Haruto protested, twitching.

“Mask’s doing the opposite, kid. You look like a suspicious assassin now.”

Grumbling, Haruto stayed outside the tavern while Garrick went in for “information gathering” (which probably meant drinking). Since his first mask was confiscated, Haruto quickly pulled out a spare one he had made earlier—a sleek black half-mask with a stylized dragon design.

He was adjusting it when chaos erupted.

A massive dragon descended on the city with a deafening roar. Crimson scales gleamed as it breathed torrents of fire, setting rooftops ablaze. Screams filled the air as people fled in panic.

Haruto’s eyes lit up with excitement and adrenaline. He threw on a black robe he’d “borrowed” earlier, pulled the dragon mask firmly over his face, and drew the practice sword Garrick had given him.

“Not on my watch!” he yelled, running toward the dragon. “Don’t worry, citizens! The Black Ninja is here!”

(The name was invented on the spot. He cringed internally but kept running.)

The dragon unleashed another blast of fire. Haruto swung his sword with the technique Garrick had drilled into him that morning. Using **Hacker**, he touched the blade and dismantled the incoming fire magic, converting it into raw mana that charged his sword with crackling energy.

*Schwing!*

The fire split apart harmlessly around him.

People stopped fleeing for a moment, staring in awe. “Who is that?!”

A high-ranking knight who had rushed to the scene narrowed his eyes at Haruto’s movements. The way he used lightning mana to boost his speed and agility… that technique was extremely advanced—something only a select few were taught directly by the royal family.

*Where did this masked boy learn the Thunder Step?* the knight wondered.

Haruto leaped onto the dragon’s back while it tried to shake him off, claws scraping against scales. Clinging tightly, he slapped his palm onto the dragon’s hide and activated **Analyze**.

Streams of complex mana code flooded his mind—vast, ancient, and incredibly dense. He spotted a weak point in the dragon’s magic circulation and attempted a **Glitch** to short-circuit it.

The dragon roared in fury.

Unfortunately, the creature had an enormous mana pool. The glitch backfired spectacularly.

**BOOOOM!**

A massive explosion ripped through the sky. The dragon detonated in a blinding blast of fire and mana, creating a crater the size of half the city. The shockwave flattened nearby buildings, and the resulting fireball could be seen from towns miles away.

Haruto barely managed to jump off and sprint away before the blast engulfed him, rolling across the ground singed and coughing.

Garrick burst out of the tavern just in time to see the devastation. His eyes widened at the giant smoking crater. Then he spotted the royal knight staring suspiciously in their direction.

Quickly pulling the kitsune mask over his own face to hide, Garrick rushed over, grabbed the somewhat injured Haruto by the collar, and dragged him into a side alley.

“You absolute moron!” the old man hissed. “What part of ‘keep a low profile’ did you not understand?!”

Haruto grinned weakly through the soot on his face, still wearing the black dragon mask. “Hey… I saved the city… technically.”

Garrick sighed deeply, already planning their hurried escape from Rivermoor before the knight could track them down.

---

chapters.title: Chapter 4: Stolen Skills and Masked Shadows
chapters.text:
While Haruto was busy turning a dragon into a walking glitch bomb, Shinji and Princess Elara were already on the road.

Their small escort traveled toward Duke City, where rumors spoke of another “Hero” who had appeared recently. Shinji rode beside the princess, still uneasy about bringing her into potential danger, but her determination left no room for argument.

---

In a quiet roadside town not far from their route, terror unfolded.

A panicked classmate sprinted through the narrow streets, breathing hard, eyes wide with fear. He tripped over a loose cobblestone and crashed to the ground.

Before he could scramble up, another figure stepped out from the shadows.

Ryota.

The tall, sharp-featured boy who had been one of Ken’s closest delinquent friends—the same group that had been bullying Takeshi the other day—looked down at his former classmate with cold eyes.

“This is as far as you go,” Ryota said flatly.

“Ryota, please stop! We’re classmates!” the boy begged, crawling backward.

Ryota didn’t listen.

An ice lance materialized in his hand. With a swift thrust, he ended the boy’s life.

**[Unique Skill: Steal Activated.]**  
New skill acquired: **Sewing**

Ryota punched the nearby wall in frustration. “What the fuck? Another trash skill?!” 

He swore under his breath. Most of his classmates had been given completely useless abilities after the summoning. So far, the only decent skills he had stolen were **Ice Magic** and **Healing** from previous victims. Everything else was garbage.

As he walked away from the body, Ryota spotted a small group of riders approaching on the main road. His eyes narrowed, then widened with greed.

One of the riders was Shinji—now with golden-blonde hair, looking every bit the summoned hero. Beside him rode a stunning girl with long crimson hair and sharp blue eyes: Princess Elara.

Ryota’s lips curled into a greedy smile. *Two heroes in one place? Their skills must be insane.*

He acted instantly.

With a wave of his hand, he froze the entire road surface into a slick sheet of ice. The horses slipped and crashed down, throwing their riders. Soldiers scrambled to their feet, drawing weapons.

Shinji was the first to recover, sword already in hand. “Ryota?! What the hell are you doing?!”

Other soldiers charged, but Ryota hurled a barrage of razor-sharp ice shards. Most of them were cut down before they could react.

Shinji stared in disbelief. “You… you were with Ken that day. The one bullying Takeshi.”

Ryota laughed. “Just… collecting skills. That’s all.”

A royal knight commander rose from the fallen horse, armor dented but eyes burning with resolve. “Leave this to me, Hero Shinji.”

The fight erupted.

Ryota formed an ice sword and clashed against the commander’s blade. He had killed dozens of monsters and several knights on his journey, stealing their magic and techniques. His movements were brutally efficient.

Shinji watched in horror as his classmate—someone he had known since middle school—fought on equal footing with a seasoned royal commander using the advanced **Lightning Step** technique.

*How did he get this strong so fast?*

Shinji found an opening and lunged, but at the last second his sword hesitated. *He’s still my classmate…*

That moment of paralysis cost him.

Princess Elara suddenly stepped forward. Lightning crackled around her as she materialized a glowing lightning sword.

Then—

**BOOOOM!**

A massive explosion echoed from far beyond the next city. A colossal pillar of fire and smoke rose into the sky—the aftermath of Haruto’s disastrous dragon glitch.

Everyone froze for a split second.

In that instant, Princess Elara moved.

Her lightning sword flashed with blinding speed. Ryota’s eyes widened, but it was already too late. His head flew from his shoulders before he could even register the attack.

Shinji stood frozen, mouth open in shock. The princess had moved so fast he couldn’t even track her movements.

Ryota’s body collapsed.

Shinji lowered his sword, voice trembling. “He… he was still our classmate…”

Princess Elara sheathed her lightning sword calmly. “He chose his path. I apologize for the trouble your classmate caused, Hero Shinji. But hesitation will only get more people killed in this world.”

Shinji could only nod sadly, guilt heavy in his chest.

---

The group continued to Duke City, where news of the distant “BOOM” had already spread.

They learned it was caused by two masked figures. One had apparently fought a rampaging dragon to protect the city before the explosion occurred.

The royal knight commander met with the local knight who had witnessed the event.

“He used the **Lightning Step** technique,” the knight reported. “Advanced level. Only those personally taught by the royal family should know it.”

The commander’s face went pale with shock. “Impossible… How could an outsider know a royal secret technique?”

Princess Elara stood quietly in the exact spot where Haruto had fought the dragon. She placed a hand over her chest, feeling a strange, familiar warmth—the same sensation she had felt on the day of the chaotic summoning.

Shinji clenched his fist. “We must find these masked people who call themselves ‘Black Ninjas.’ They could be friend… or foe.”

---

In a hidden safehouse on the outskirts of the ruined city, Haruto slowly woke up on a simple cot.

He blinked, looking around the unfamiliar room. Garrick was in the corner, speaking in low voices with a few rough-looking men.

“…and the king must never find out about this,” Garrick was saying.

Haruto sat up quickly. “Wait, old man! You’re not doing something illegal, are you? I have zero intention of becoming an international criminal!”

Garrick sighed deeply and turned around. “Fine. Sit down and listen carefully, kid. This is for the benefit of the country.”

Haruto crossed his arms, still wary. “This better be good.”

---

At the Duke’s grand palace, Shinji and Princess Elara finally arrived.

A girl was sitting elegantly at a long table, sipping tea with perfect posture. She looked up as they entered.

Shinji’s eyes widened in recognition.

“You’re… the class rep!”

---

chapters.title: Chapter 5: Seeds of Domination
chapters.text:
Ken strolled down a dusty street in a remote border town, orange hair catching the sunlight. He was still riding high from the power of his **Blast** skill—every punch an explosion, every fight an easy victory. Life in this new world felt good.

Until a familiar figure stepped out from an alley and blocked his path.

Takeshi.

The nerd who had once been their favorite punching bag now stood with bright red hair and an icy, emotionless stare.

Ken smirked. “Oh? Look who’s here. Still alive, huh?”

Takeshi didn’t smile back. His eyes glowed with dark power.

**[Dominate Skill Activated.]**

Ken suddenly felt an unbearable weight crush down on his mind. His thoughts grew sluggish, his will buckling under an invisible force. His knees trembled.

Takeshi spoke calmly, voice cold as steel. “This is for everything you people did to me. From now on, you will be my slave. You will help me dominate this entire world.”

Ken’s proud delinquent ego fought desperately… then shattered. His eyes went blank. He dropped to one knee, head bowed.

“I will do as you say… Master.”

Takeshi’s lips curled into a satisfied, evil smile. “Good boy.”

---

In the rival Kingdom of Valthor, a tense meeting was underway in the throne room.

A spy knelt before the king, head lowered. “We interfered with Aldoria’s summoning ritual, but we could not fully stop it. The heroes were scattered across their lands. However, as long as the three prophesied heroes do not gather together, they pose no real threat.”

The king nodded grimly. “Very well. We will launch our attack before they can unite.”

The crown prince leaned forward with a cunning grin. “But wait… I want Princess Elara secured and brought to me.”

His younger sister, the second princess with sleek black hair and piercing red eyes, snorted in amusement. She was the same age as Elara and burned with jealousy whenever the Aldorian princess’s beauty was praised in other countries.

“Come on, big brother. You can find another girl to play with. But if you really insist… I can crush her, bring her to you as a gift. You won’t mind if one or two arms or legs get broken, right?”

The prince laughed heartily. “Alright, alright. At least try to bring her in one piece. Once I’ve had my fun with her, you can do whatever you want.”

The second princess’s red eyes narrowed with dark pleasure. She hated how Elara was admired everywhere while their own kingdom—full of shady dealings and dark rumors—struggled to form alliances. It wasn’t that she wasn’t beautiful herself… it was simply that no one trusted Valthor.

---

Back in the Kingdom of Aldoria, King Reginald sat at the war council table, face heavy with worry.

His eldest son asked, “Father, what should we do about the third hero? He is still missing. Only those of royal blood can sense whether a person is truly one of the summoned heroes.”

King Reginald sighed. “We keep searching. But tensions with the neighboring kingdoms are rising dangerously high. We must prepare for war.”

---

In Duke City, inside a quiet room in the duke’s palace, Shinji sat across from the class representative, Rin.

Rin had always been the serious, studious girl in their class. Now her hair had a subtle scholarly sheen, and she carried herself with calm confidence.

“So… how did you end up here?” Shinji asked curiously.

Rin smiled faintly, sipping her tea. “It’s obvious, isn’t it? I love studying. When the summoning light hit, I was in the school library surrounded by books. My unique skill, **Wisdom**, lets me absorb knowledge at an incredible speed. I’ve already learned several complex magic systems in just a few days.”

She paused, then added, “My rapid progress caught the attention of the local mages. Word reached the duke—who happens to be King Reginald’s younger brother. That’s why I’m here now.”

Shinji nodded, impressed. “Your skill sounds really useful. We need every advantage we can get.”

---

In a dark alley far from the palace, a figure moved silently.

Ryota walked with a slight limp, but his wounds were already closing thanks to the stolen **Healing** skill. He remembered the moment he had killed the princess’s escort knights—one of them had been a mage. He had stolen **Illusion Magic** right before escaping death.

He had appraised Shinji during the fight and found him… underwhelming. But he hadn’t bothered to fully appraise the royal knight commander. That man was a real beast.

Ryota’s eyes gleamed with dark ambition as he thought of Princess Elara.

“She won’t go gently into death… but I’ll make sure to take everything she has.”

---

chapters.title: Chapter 6: Telepathic Hack and Unexpected Reunion
chapters.text:
In the opulent halls of Count Patrick’s palace, the count sat at his grand desk, carefully writing a sealed letter. A trusted messenger stood waiting nearby.

Suddenly, a calm voice cut through the silence.

“Are you done with the letter?”

Guards drew their weapons instantly. “Who’s there?!”

From the shadows stepped a figure in a sleek black robe and dragon mask — Haruto.

“Black Ninja,” he announced casually.

Count Patrick’s eyes widened. “Black… Ninja? How did you enter my palace without triggering any alarms?!”

Haruto shrugged. “Top hackers disable the antivirus before infecting the system. In your case, it was the barrier magic. Took a little effort, but I managed somehow.”
