The count opened his mouth to shout for more guards, but Haruto moved like lightning — using the **Lightning Step** technique Garrick had taught him. In a blur, he knocked out both the count and the messenger with precise strikes, then dragged their unconscious bodies out of the palace.

Nearby, at a modest inn on the outskirts, Garrick was still meticulously planning an infiltration when a window suddenly knocked open. Two bodies were unceremoniously dropped inside.

Garrick stared in stunned silence.

Haruto dusted off his hands. “Your planning was too slow for my patience.”

The old man quickly read the letter the count had been writing. His face darkened.

“They’re planning to abduct the princess… with traitors inside the royal palace.”

Haruto nodded. “Alright. I’m heading to the royal palace now.”

Garrick stopped him. “Wait — how will you recognize the traitor?”

Haruto tapped the side of his head with a smirk. “Once you make the count open his mouth, tell me everything. I’ll reach the capital and we can talk telepathically. I’ll keep the line open.”

Garrick was stunned. “Telepathically…? How far have you pushed that skill already?”

Haruto grinned under the mask. As someone who already understood radio waves, signal encoding, and data transmission, all he had to do was replace technology with magic. Mana made an excellent carrier wave.

“Leave it to me, old man.”

With that, Haruto vanished into the night, heading toward the capital at high speed.

---

In a certain lively town, outside a bustling brothel, Ken knelt on all fours like a dog, thick rope tied around his neck.

Takeshi, red hair shining with wicked confidence, was inside enjoying the company of several beautiful girls, laughing loudly. Every so often he would tug the rope, forcing Ken to bark or perform humiliating tricks for the amusement of onlookers.

Ken’s proud delinquent spirit was completely gone — buried under the overwhelming power of **Dominate**.

---

At the royal palace in Aldoria’s capital, Rin had finally arrived with Shinji and Princess Elara.

The first thing the class rep did was head straight for the royal library, eyes sparkling with excitement as she devoured ancient tomes with her **Wisdom** skill.

Later, while Shinji went to the knights’ training grounds to hone his **Justice** skill, Rin and Princess Elara sat together in a sunny courtyard, drinking tea and having a light, girly chat.

A soft knock sounded on the door.

A woman in her twenties entered — the kingdom’s top royal mage, elegant and sharp-eyed.

“Forgive the interruption, Your Highness. I wanted to ask if any of you know anything about the magic that caused that giant crater in the border city the other day.”

Before anyone could answer, Princess Elara suddenly placed a hand over her chest. A familiar, warm sensation pulsed inside her — the same feeling from the summoning day.

Without a word, she stood up and began walking, as if drawn by an invisible thread.

---

Deep in a western forest, a chubby classmate was sleeping peacefully against a tree, snoring loudly.

A massive dragon landed nearby, drooling as it prepared to make a meal of the sleeping boy.

Just as the dragon opened its jaws, a drop of saliva landed on the boy’s face.

He woke up instantly.

“Oh? Food came to me this time?”

A few minutes later, only a pile of cleanly picked bones remained of the dragon. The chubby boy — Chojoro — licked his fingers happily.

Another classmate emerged from the bushes. “So you’re hungry as always, Chojoro.”

Chojoro looked up. “Oh, it’s you, Yuta.”

The two talked briefly. Yuta explained his plan while Chojoro listened, still chewing on a dragon rib.

Chojoro’s unique skill was **Gluttony** — he could eat literally anything and convert it into power.

Yuta made a simple proposal: “Join me. I’ll provide you with unlimited food.”

Chojoro agreed without a second thought. “Deal!”

---

Haruto finally reached the capital. Using a quick **Hex** hack on the outer barrier, he slipped inside without triggering any alarms.

The moment his foot crossed the threshold, however, he felt someone behind him.

He turned.

A beautiful girl with long crimson hair stood there — Princess Elara.

Haruto tilted his head. “Who are you?”

She didn’t reply. Instead, she walked straight up to him and pulled him into a tight hug, whispering softly against his chest:

“I finally found you…”

Haruto’s face turned bright red under the mask. “W-what happened—?!”

Before he could process the sudden embrace, another calm voice cut through the moment.

“So… you are here too, Haruto.”

A cold shiver ran down Haruto’s spine.

He looked over and saw a girl with neat pink hair and glasses standing a few steps away — it was none other than the class rep, Rin.

She adjusted her glasses with a small smile.

“You came all the way here without completing your homework? Shinji told me that.”

Haruto froze, completely speechless for the first time since arriving in this world.

---

chapters.title: Chapter 7: Decompiled Flames
chapters.text:
Himari walked alone along a quiet forest path, her purple hair swaying gently with each step. A wide river soon blocked her way. On the opposite bank, a group of travelers stood helplessly — the old stone bridge had collapsed into the rushing water.

The people waved desperately, asking for help. Himari didn’t even slow down. Her hand rested lightly on the hilt of her katana.

Before anyone could react, she drew her blade in one fluid motion.

*Schwing!*

The water split cleanly in two, forming a temporary path of dry riverbed as if the river itself had been sliced apart. Himari crossed without a single word or glance, sheathing her sword the moment she reached the other side.

The travelers stood dumbfounded, mouths open. They quickly hurried across the temporary gap before the waters crashed back together. They tried to thank her, bowing repeatedly, but Himari paid them no mind and simply continued walking, cold and distant as ever.

---

In the rival Kingdom of Valthor, war preparations were in full swing. Blacksmiths hammered armor, soldiers drilled in formation, and supply wagons rolled endlessly through the gates.

A figure in a black robe approached the throne room — Yuta.

His unique skill, **Greed**, had shown him a clear truth: he would never gain true glory or power as long as the three main prophesied heroes remained alive. So he had chosen the smarter path — joining the enemy nation for his own benefit.

The Valthor king and his advisors listened with growing surprise as Yuta spoke. He knew far more than their best spies.

“I know the three heroes well,” Yuta said calmly. “Shinji, Rin, and Haruto. I can tell you their abilities.”

He revealed everything he had learned — Shinji’s **Justice** skill that grew stronger with people’s hope, Rin’s **Wisdom** that let her absorb knowledge instantly, and Haruto’s mysterious **Hacker** skill.

“Shinji won’t be much trouble,” Yuta continued. “He hates underhanded tactics and is easy to manipulate or kill in the right situation. Rin and Haruto will be far more bothersome. Especially Haruto.”

He explained how another classmate, Kaeri (who could read minds), had tried to probe Haruto but ran into what felt like an always-active antivirus — Haruto’s hacking skill constantly on high alert.

“Currently, I have four classmates with me,” Yuta added. “Including Chojoro with his **Gluttony** and Kaeri.”

The Valthor king smiled darkly. “You will be well rewarded for this information.”

---

Back in the Kingdom of Aldoria’s royal palace, Haruto sat stiffly on a luxurious sofa. Princess Elara sat unusually close beside him, her crimson hair brushing his shoulder. King Reginald, Shinji, and Rin were also present.

Rin did nothing but stare intently at how close the princess had gotten to Haruto.

Shinji finally broke the awkward silence. “It’s been a while, Haruto. I never expected you to be one of the heroes.”

Rin pushed up her glasses. “Yes, it is obvious. Someone like him cannot possibly be a real hero. There must be some mistake.”

Shinji smirked. “Shouldn’t you be reading books in the library? Up until now you never cared about meetings, but now you’re here. Oh, don’t tell me — you can’t leave Haruto out of your sight. Even when he wasn’t here, you talked about him a lot.”

Rin’s face turned bright red. “I was never—! Shut up!”

Haruto’s heart pounded wildly. *What the hell is going on here?!*

King Reginald cleared his throat. “Haruto, do you know anything about the giant crater that appeared in the border city recently?”

Haruto shot up from the sofa. “No! That wasn’t me! It was a dragon that… self-exploded.”

Everyone except Princess Elara looked shocked.

“So it really was you…” Shinji muttered.

Elara simply smiled softly, as if she had expected it all along.

---

The next moment, they were all standing in the royal training grounds.

“Why did you bring me here?” Haruto asked, annoyed.

Rin adjusted her glasses with a serious expression. “It is to teach you a lesson about carelessness.”

The royal knight commander stepped forward. “Mock battle between Hero Haruto and Hero Rin — begin!”

Haruto glanced at Shinji for help, but Shinji just looked away awkwardly, pretending he couldn’t do anything.

Rin began chanting calmly: “O fire, come forth! Incinerate my enemy with your flames!”

As she chanted, Haruto quietly activated a barrier he called **Memory Viewer**. In his mind, all magic appeared as clean binary code and mana structures. He could see every change happening inside Rin as well.

Rin finished her chant and released a giant fireball.

Haruto dodged effortlessly with **Lightning Step**, moving in a burst of crackling speed.

King Reginald’s eyes widened in amazement. “He can really use the Lightning Step…!”

Haruto smirked. “Function is ready.”

He simply said “Fireball” — and an identical giant fireball appeared in his hand, launched straight at Rin without any incantation.

Rin was taken aback. “How can he use it without chanting?!”

She quickly tried to chant a shield spell, but it was too late. The fireball was already flying toward her.

Shinji suddenly stepped in, blocking the spell with his sword. He had learned from the fight with Ryota — never space out in battle.

Rin yelled in disbelief, “How did you copy my spell?! Or rather, how did you even use it without incantation?!”

Haruto shrugged casually, as if it were the most obvious thing in the world.

“I decompiled your magic and created a function — like in Python. Now I can call it anytime without the full incantation.”

The entire training ground fell silent.

Rin stared at him, glasses slightly askew, face a mix of shock and frustration.

---

chapters.title: Chapter 8: Virus in the Palace
chapters.text:
The training grounds fell into stunned silence after Haruto casually explained how he had decompiled and turned Rin’s fireball into a reusable Python-like function.

King Reginald, however, couldn’t hold back any longer. His eyes were fixed on Haruto with intense curiosity.

“Where exactly did you learn the Lightning Step technique?”

Haruto scratched his white hair casually. “I was taught by an old man. What was his name again… Gar— um, Garrick. Yeah, Garrick.”

A collective shout echoed through the training area.

“WHAAAAAT?!”

The royal knight commander stepped forward, voice shaking. “So… he really survived.”

King Reginald’s face paled with shock and something like relief. “It was my father who found him during the summoning chaos. No wonder you know so much. Garrick was the former king — my father. He was supposed to have died in the last major assassination attempt, but he survived and went into hiding to root out the traitors within the kingdom.”

The king looked at Haruto urgently. “Where is he now?”

“He’s making Count Patrick confess the names of the other traitors,” Haruto replied.

The king nodded. “Has he told you anything yet?”

“Not directly,” Haruto said, then smirked under his serious expression. “But I already found the traitors.”

He held up a small, wriggling red worm-like construct made of pure mana.

Shinji tilted his head. “What is that?”

“It’s a virus. Magic malware I created to scan and infect. I found three of them active in this palace.” Haruto pointed toward a maid standing nervously near the wall. “And one of them is her.”

The moment the words left his mouth, the maid’s eyes widened in panic. She bolted for the door.

Too late.

Haruto had already raised a silent barrier, sealing every exit. The maid slammed into the invisible wall and collapsed.

“From the moment I first stepped into the capital,” Haruto explained calmly, “I was already running scans and blocking escape routes.”

He continued, “I also found someone else from our class — Kaeri. She tried to read my mind to steal knowledge, but my Hacker skill blocked her like an antivirus and reverse-tracked her location. She’s currently in the Valthor Kingdom.”

Everyone in the room froze in shock.

“Our own classmates… chose to oppose us,” Shinji muttered, fists clenched.

Princess Elara’s blue eyes darkened with quiet anger.

---

Meanwhile, in a bustling town not far from the capital, Minato — blue hair messy from endless dungeon grinding — finally arrived, dreaming of the classic isekai life.

*Finally! Time to meet the princess, build a harem, and live the protagonist dream!*

His mind was practically drooling at the thought.

Suddenly, he spotted a familiar sign. “Oh… it’s a brothel.”

He shook his head firmly. “No! I’m the protagonist of this isekai anime. I mustn’t do anything like that. What would the viewers think if they saw me?!”

He was about to turn away when he noticed Ken tied up outside like a dog, barking on command.

Curious, Minato walked closer.

Then Takeshi stepped out of the brothel, red hair gleaming, looking completely at ease.

Minato’s eyes widened. “Of all people… Takeshi?!”

But the moment he met Takeshi’s cold, domineering gaze, a chill ran down his spine.

Takeshi unleashed his **Dominate** skill, sending a heavy wave of mental pressure toward Minato.

Minato resisted, pushing back with his own growing power. “It… won’t work on me!”

Takeshi’s lips curled. “Interesting. You managed to resist.”

He fired several dark energy balls at Minato, hoping to weaken him. At the same time, he ordered Ken to attack as well.

Minato began fighting back, copying whatever minor skills he could, but at his current level he couldn’t fully steal their powerful abilities yet.

The battle raged violently in the street, drawing panicked screams from onlookers as spells and explosions lit up the area.

---

Back at the royal palace, after the traitors had been rounded up and dealt with, Haruto was sleeping soundly in a luxurious guest bed, exhausted from the day’s events.

Princess Elara sat quietly beside him, gently watching his peaceful sleeping face, her crimson hair glowing softly in the moonlight.

The door creaked open. Class rep Rin sneaked inside, trying to be stealthy.

She froze when she saw Elara already there.

“P-Princess?! Why are you here?!”

Elara giggled softly. “Just doing what you came here for?”

Rin’s eyes shot open wide, face turning scarlet. “N-No! I didn’t come to peep on him or anything like that!”

The princess only giggled more.

Then her expression suddenly turned cold and serious. She stood up and walked right up to Rin, leaning close.

“Dear Rin… I have decided to become his wife. I don’t mind if you become his second or third or whatever, but I won’t appreciate it if you bad-mouth him. Got it?”

Rin tried to protest. “S-Second or third—?!”

But when she looked into Elara’s eyes, she realized the princess was completely serious.

Elara gave one last gentle smile and left the room, closing the door softly behind her.

Rin stood there for a long moment, heart racing. Then she slowly walked closer to the sleeping Haruto. Her cheeks burned bright red as she stared at his white hair and relaxed face.

She whispered very quietly, almost to herself, “Idiot…”

---

chapters.title: Chapter 9: Morning Chaos and Charmed Betrayal
chapters.text:
Haruto’s eyes snapped open to the feeling of warmth on both sides of him.

He froze.

Princess Elara was sleeping peacefully on his right, her crimson hair spilled across the pillow like silk. On his left, Class Rep Rin was curled up with her pink hair slightly messy, glasses neatly placed on the bedside table, one hand lightly clutching the edge of his sleeve.

Haruto’s brain short-circuited.

“WHAT THE HELL?!”

He bolted upright, yelling at the top of his lungs.

Elara stirred first, blinking sleepily before a soft, radiant smile spread across her face.

“Oh… good morning, my dearest husband~”

She leaned in as if to kiss his cheek.

Haruto’s face exploded into a deep shade of red. “H-HUSBAND?! Since when—?!”

Before he could finish the sentence, Rin woke up with a startled squeak. The moment she realized the situation, her entire face turned scarlet. She let out a high-pitched “Kyaa—!” and scrambled off the bed, running toward the door while covering her face with both hands.

“I-I didn’t mean to fall asleep here! This is all a misunderstanding!” she cried, slamming the door behind her as she fled down the hallway.

Elara giggled, clearly amused, and hugged Haruto’s arm tighter. “She’s so cute when she’s flustered.”

Haruto’s heart was hammering so hard he thought it might explode. *This isekai is broken… way too many flags at once!*

Then, suddenly, something pinged in his mind.

His magical malware — the red worm virus he had released across the palace — had spread far beyond the capital walls. It had reached a group of shady figures in dark robes hiding in a forest clearing several kilometers away. Through the virus link, he could see fragments of their conversation and mana signatures.

“Someone’s watching us…” one of the robed figures muttered.

Haruto narrowed his eyes. “Trouble incoming.”

---

Meanwhile, outside the town, the fierce battle between Minato and Takeshi was still raging.

Explosions shook the street. Minato was breathing hard, copying whatever small skills he could while dodging Ken’s explosive punches and Takeshi’s dark energy blasts.

Suddenly, a seductive voice echoed from above.

“My my… look who’s here.”

A beautiful woman in revealing robes descended gracefully. Horns curved elegantly from her head and a thin tail swayed behind her — a succubus clearly sent by the Valthor Kingdom.

She smiled with dangerous charm and released a powerful wave of pink mist.

**Charm Magic: Full Activation.**

Both Takeshi and Ken froze, eyes glazing over as the succubus’s control took hold.

Minato, however, felt the mental intrusion and immediately recognized the danger. “Not good—!”

He broke free at the last second and sprinted away as fast as his legs could carry him.

Behind him, dozens of townspeople and hidden agents who had already fallen under the succubus’s charm suddenly attacked, eyes blank and movements mechanical.

Minato barely managed to escape, using every copied skill he had to create distractions and slip through the crowd. He disappeared into the forest, heart pounding.

But Takeshi and Ken remained completely under her control now, kneeling obediently before the succubus.

She licked her lips with satisfaction. “Two new toys for the kingdom… how delightful.”

---

Back at the royal palace, Haruto gently pried himself from Elara’s hug and stood up, expression serious.

“Something’s wrong. My virus just picked up movement outside the capital. And I think one of our classmates is already in trouble.”

Princess Elara’s playful demeanor vanished instantly, replaced by the sharp gaze of a royal who understood the weight of war.

“Then we move,” she said firmly. “Together.”

Haruto nodded, white hair falling over his eyes as he activated his **Hacker** interface in his mind, already preparing new countermeasures.

The real war was about to begin.
